new_classes.label_en: Blended malt Scotch whisky in containers > 2 L (subheading 2208 30 49)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Malt Scotch Whisky in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 Litern (Unterposition 2208 30 49)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended malt Scotch whisky in containers holding more than 2 litres, produced by blending two or more single malt Scotch whiskies distilled at different distilleries.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the three primary distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) blended malt composition quality (blend of two or more single malt whiskies from different distilleries), (2) Scotch whisky origin quality (produced and matured in Scotland), and (3) container volume > 2 L (LargeContainer, which maps to ≥ 2 L in the TBox — a minor precision gap since the CN boundary is strictly > 2 L, but no dedicated strictly-greater-than-2L container class exists in the TBox beyond LargeContainer). The blending process restriction further reinforces the production method. A disjointness complement with the sibling subheading 22083041 (containers ≤ 2 L) is included. Coverage is set to 0.9 rather than 1.0 because the TBox LargeContainer class uses ≥ 2 L (inclusive) while the CN text implies > 2 L (exclusive), and because the exact Scotch Whisky geographic/legal production requirements (Scottish origin, maturation period, etc.) are only partially captured through the existing ScotchWhiskyOriginQuality quality class.
